# Holo Forensics v0.1.6

## ✨ What's Changed

- Added a live Windows collection for Microsoft Protection Logs (`MPLog*.log`) that stages Defender Support logs through the existing archive workflow with VSS-backed collection, SHA-256 verification, and no duplicate registry or EVTX coverage.
- Added a native `windows_mplogs` parser for Microsoft Defender Support logs, including UTF-8 and UTF-16LE decoding, raw-line preservation, cautious continuation handling, normalized field extraction, and timestamp assumption tracking for timezone-free log lines.
- Wired the new MPLogs flow through the CLI, desktop collection catalog, parser catalog, and offline parse planning so collected packages can be parsed end-to-end without custom lab steps.
- Updated the README and wiki collection/parser references to document the new Microsoft Protection Logs coverage and its collection and parse contracts.

## 🧪 Release Notes

- Compared with [v0.1.5](https://github.com/false00/holoForensics/releases/tag/v0.1.5), this release adds end-to-end Microsoft Defender Support MPLogs coverage: Holo Forensics can now collect `C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\Windows Defender\Support\MPLog*.log` from a live system and parse the resulting package directly into JSONL.
- The new parser is intentionally defensive for DFIR use. It treats MPLogs as semi-structured text instead of EVTX, preserves each raw line even when only part of a record can be normalized, and records timestamp assumptions when the source log omits timezone information.
- Documentation and catalog surfaces were updated in the same change so the new collector and parser appear consistently in the desktop workflow, CLI help, README coverage tables, and wiki indexes.
